execution_details.stdout:
# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in
# /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there. To override
# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.
#
# For more information, see sysctl.conf(5) and sysctl.d(5).

scan_output: Vulnerability sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_accept_source_route not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message:
We have finished.**OpenSCAP finding remediation completed.**

- Added `/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-source-route.conf` with:
  ```
  net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
  ```
- Reloaded sysctl (`sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-source-route.conf`) to apply the change immediately.

The kernel parameter is now permanently disabled; the setting will persist across reboots. No duplicate or conflicting entries exist, and the change is reversible by editing or removing the file.
